releases.description:
## MCP Connector 0.10.0 — Vault-native prompt library

Your Obsidian vault is now your prompt editor.

### What's new

**MCP Prompts — vault-native prompt library** (ADR-0006)

Create a `Prompts/` folder at the root of your vault, drop a markdown file in it tagged `#mcp-tools-prompt`, and it immediately appears as an MCP prompt in every compatible client — Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, VS Code, and any other client that speaks `prompts/list` + `prompts/get`.

**Writing a prompt takes 30 seconds:**

```markdown
---
tags:
  - mcp-tools-prompt
description: Summarize my recent notes on a topic
---

Summarize my notes from the past <% tp.mcpTools.prompt("days", "How many days to look back, e.g. 7") %> days
about **<% tp.mcpTools.prompt("topic", "The subject, e.g. 'writing habits'") %>**.

Give me the three most recurring themes and one action item.
```

The `<% tp.mcpTools.prompt("name", "desc") %>` lines declare the parameters — they are stripped from the rendered output and their values injected wherever `{{name}}` appears in the body. The client asks for each value once.

**No Templater required.** The renderer runs entirely in-process. Any other Templater expressions in the body pass through verbatim — the plugin only processes `tp.mcpTools.prompt()` declarations and `{{placeholder}}` substitutions.

**Live discovery.** Adding, renaming, or deleting a prompt file updates the list immediately. No restart, no reconnect.

**Client integration:**
- **Claude Desktop**: prompts appear under *Attach from MCP* → `mcp-tools-istefox`
- **Claude Code**: slash commands `/mcp__mcp-tools-istefox__<prompt-name>`
- **Cursor / Cline / Windsurf / VS Code**: any client supporting `prompts/list` + `prompts/get`

Full reference: [`docs/features/prompt-system.md`](docs/features/prompt-system.md)

---

releases.description:
## MCP Connector 0.9.0 — Multilingual semantic search

Semantic search now works properly on non-Latin vaults.

### What's new

**Multilingual embedding providers with download-on-demand**

The native MiniLM-L6-v2 model (default since 0.4.x) struggles with Arabic, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and other non-Latin scripts. 0.9.0 adds two new ONNX providers available on demand:

| Provider | Size | Best for |
|---|---|---|
| **Gemma 300M** (`embedding-gemma-300m`) | ~300 MB | Non-Latin scripts (Arabic, CJK, Hebrew, Thai, …) |
| **Multilingual-E5-Base** (`multilingual-e5-base`) | ~270 MB | Mixed-language vaults |
| Native MiniLM-L6-v2 | ~25 MB | English / Latin-script vaults (default, unchanged) |

All providers:
- Download from HuggingFace on first selection, cached locally
- **Swap live** — the previous provider keeps answering queries while the new index builds in the background
- Each provider keeps its own isolated index directory — switching doesn't destroy your existing index

**Auto-suggestion.** On startup the plugin samples up to 50 notes for non-ASCII character ratio. If > 30% of characters are non-ASCII, a banner recommends switching to Gemma. No automatic switch — the decision is yours.

**`find_broken_links` — new `scope` parameter.** Pass vault-relative path prefixes to limit the scan to a folder subtree. Without `scope`, the scan remains vault-wide. Addresses payload overflow on large vaults.

**Chunker enhancements.** Frontmatter is now a dedicated `#frontmatter` chunk for independent embedding; H3 sub-sections split before the sliding-window fallback; 1-sentence overlap at embed time (stored text unchanged, so delta detection stays stable).

---

### Upgrading from a pre-0.4.9 version? One-time step if Claude shows "Server disconnected"

0.4.9 fixes a bug where the in-process MCP server could fail to release its port on Obsidian reload/disable and "walk" to the next one (27200 → 27201 → …). If you ran a pre-0.4.9 build, your `claude_desktop_config.json` may still point at a walked port from that era while 0.4.9 now correctly binds the default port — so Claude Desktop connects to the wrong port and reports **"MCP mcp-tools-istefox: Server disconnected"**.

**Fix (one time): fully quit Claude Desktop (Cmd+Q on macOS) and reopen it.** Claude Desktop only re-reads `claude_desktop_config.json` at launch — closing the window or an in-app restart is not enough. With auto-write enabled (the default) the plugin keeps the config in sync from here on.

If it still fails after a full Claude restart:
- Check the plugin's server port matches the config. The plugin logs its port on start (Settings → MCP Connector → Open Logs); the config entry is `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/mcp`.
- Make sure only one Obsidian vault has the plugin enabled (two instances contend for the port).
- Fully restart Claude Desktop again.

This is a one-time migration artifact from the old port-walk bug, not a recurring 0.4.9 issue — fresh 0.4.9 installs are unaffected.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.4.4 — Graph navigation feature batch

Cycle 5 of the 0.4.x line: **4 new MCP tools** (one external-contributor, three Stefano-authored) + a schema caveat refinement. Folotp's round-5 sentinel verified the 0.4.3 patch family is structurally closed (`#80` / `#81` / `#84` reject guards working end-to-end), so this cut is feature-only.

## What's new

- **`list_tags`** — every tag in the vault with usage count. Sourced from `app.metadataCache.getTags()`, no plugin dependency. ([#83](https://github.com/istefox/obsidian-mcp-connector/pull/83) by [@marcoaperez](https://github.com/marcoaperez), first external-contributor PR landed on the fork)
- **`get_files_by_tag`** — sibling of `list_tags`. Tag with or without `#`, case-insensitive. `includeNested` flag (default `true`) matches `#project` against `#project/active`, mirroring Obsidian's tag pane. Counts inline + frontmatter occurrences as separate hits for relevance ranking.
- **`get_outgoing_links`** — first member of the new **"Links" section**. Body links + embeds + frontmatter links emanating from a file. `targetPath` resolved via `metadataCache.getFirstLinkpathDest` so callers don't need a separate round-trip to resolve linkpaths into vault paths.
- **`get_backlinks`** — every file that links to the given target, with per-source count. `includeUnresolved` opt-in for broken-link auditing. Does not error if target was deleted — backlinks routinely outlive their target after a delete or rename.
- **`patch_vault_file` schema caveat** — `createTargetIfMissing` `.describe()` now flags the H2-root silent-create branch upfront via `tools/list`, surfaced after folotp's round-5 observation on vaults using a "title in frontmatter, body starts at H2" layout. Default behaviour unchanged.

**24 MCP tools total** (was 21).

## Quality

- Plugin tools test suite: **178/178 green** (was 140/140 — +38 cases across the 3 new tools + edge-case coverage)
- `bun run check`: clean across all 4 packages
- CI release run: [`25393505832`](https://github.com/istefox/obsidian-mcp-connector/actions/runs/25393505832), green ~32s

## Install

- **BRAT** (available now): updates automatically — pin is `istefox/obsidian-mcp-connector`.
- **Community store**: pending acceptance of [`obsidianmd/obsidian-releases#11919`](https://github.com/obsidianmd/obsidian-releases/pull/11919) (currently in week 3 of review).

## Cycle history

`0.4.0-beta.3 → 0.4.0 → 0.4.1 → 0.4.2 → 0.4.3 → 0.4.4`. Five cycles in. Cycle 4 (`#80` H2-root reject + `#81` block-in-table reject + `#84` block-in-fenced reject) closed structurally on the `patch_vault_file` safety surface; cycle 5 is feature-only.
